Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
multi-account Gmail/Calendar, server shell skill, CLI spinner, Sentry fixes

- Multi-account Google: support up to 5 Gmail + Calendar accounts via
  GOOGLE_ACCOUNT_{2-5}_* env vars; CalendarReadSkill queries all accounts
  by default; skills/dispatcher pass account name through all flows

- ServerShellSkill: full shell access on the server \(navigate, mkdir,
  builds, git, docker, npm/pip\); safe commands run immediately, destructive
  commands \(rm -rf, kill, etc.\) require confirmation; forbidden disk ops
  hard-blocked; 120s timeout + 8k output truncation

- CLI spinner: animated braille spinner with time-based phase labels
  \(Thinking → Routing intent → Running skill → Composing response\) runs
  in background thread while API call is in flight; done banner shows
  intent + agent + elapsed time; Ctrl+C handled cleanly

- Sentry: fix LoggingIntegration\(level=None\) that was flooding every
  report with INFO breadcrumbs; changed to WARNING+; add
  ClientConnectionResetError \(Slack SDK reconnect race\) to before_send
  ignore list; add before_breadcrumb to drop httplib noise; cap
  max_breadcrumbs=40; tighten start_socket_mode error handler

- .env.example: complete rewrite covering all config variables including
  Qdrant, Sentry, IONOS, Twilio, Telos, cost/rate limits, memory,
  Flower/Grafana, SSH keys, and full multi-account Google template

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)"
2>&1)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: sentry auto-investigate and fix via LLM + Celery

When Sentry sends a warning/error/critical/fatal webhook the Brain now:
  1. Creates a pending_write_task with appropriate severity level
  2. Immediately dispatches a Celery task \(investigate_and_fix_sentry_issue\)
  3. Fetches the full stack trace from Sentry API
  4. Asks Claude Haiku for a structured JSON fix plan \(fixable, patches, commit_message\)
  5. Applies file patches via RepoClient → commits → pushes if fixable
  6. Resolves the issue in Sentry if fully addressed
  7. Posts a detailed Slack summary to brain-alerts

Also includes supporting updates to celery_app, evals reporter,
skill_discovery, nginx config, grafana dashboard, and docker-compose
from previous session work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git add app/integrations/contacts.py && git commit -m "fix: contacts integration — replace postgres.fetch\(\) with postgres.execute\(\)

postgres module only exposes execute\(\) not fetch\(\). All ContactsClient
methods were calling the non-existent fetch\(\) method, causing contacts_read
and contacts_write to fail entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push origin main 2>&1)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Claude Code-style CLI, task board, self-update routing, cross-interface memory

CLI \(brain.py\):
- Markdown renderer: word-wrap prose, render **bold**, `code`, headers, bullets, code blocks
- Claude Code-style response header: ◆ Brain  [intent]  Xs + full-width separator
- Terminal-width-aware layout using shutil.get_terminal_size\(\)
- Cleaner banner: Brain ◆ url / session+approval / env+branch+help divider
- /help Self-modification section with read/update/deploy command hints

Brain self-update routing:
- intent.py: "update the CLI", "edit brain.py", "update your code" → server_shell read_file
- llm_router.py DEFAULT_AGENT_PROMPT: self-modification block — brain uses server_shell
  to read files itself, never asks user to paste code or run cat commands

Task board \(app/router/task_board.py, app/skills/task_skill.py\):
- REST CRUD at /api/v1/board/tasks with priority, approval_level, tags, assigned_to
- TaskCreate/Read/Update skills registered in dispatcher

Cross-interface memory & autonomy:
- All interfaces share primary "brain" session
- BRAIN_AUTONOMY=true skips confirmation for all pending actions
- Brain container has Docker socket + live host code at /sentinel-project

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
feat: CI/CD GitOps loop — GHCR build, webhook deploy, AI PR workflow

- Add .github/workflows/release-image.yml — triggers after CI passes on
  main, builds and pushes ghcr.io/cocacolasante/sentinel:latest + sha tag,
  then POSTs signed deploy webhook to sentinelai.cloud
- Add deploy/webhook_server.py — stdlib HTTP server on port 9000, verifies
  X-Deploy-Secret header, spawns deploy.sh as a subprocess
- Add deploy/deploy.sh — pulls new image from GHCR, restarts brain /
  celery-worker / celery-beat / flower via docker compose
- Update Dockerfile — install gh CLI for AI PR creation inside container,
  add OCI labels so GHCR links package to repo
- Update docker-compose.yml — image: tags on all built services, GH_TOKEN
  forwarded to brain container, new deploy-agent service \(python:3.11-slim,
  stays alive during brain restarts, mounts docker.sock + deploy/\)
- Update nginx/nginx.conf — /webhooks/ location block proxied to
  deploy-agent:9000
- Update app/brain/llm_router.py — extend DEFAULT_AGENT_PROMPT with 8-step
  safe PR workflow \(branch → commit → push → gh pr create → auto-merge,
  never push directly to main\)
- Add CICD.md — full setup and configuration guide \(9 steps, verification
  checklist, troubleshooting section\)
- Update README.md — add CI/CD section linking to CICD.md
- Add milestone_logger, task board, tests, pyproject.toml, CI workflow and
  other session changes carried in this commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(git add tests/conftest.py .claude/settings.local.json)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: add dummy Slack env vars to conftest so CI tests pass without credentials

AsyncApp in app/router/slack.py is instantiated at module import time.
When SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is absent/empty slack_bolt raises BoltError before
any test patch can intercept it, causing all tests to fail in CI.

Set dummy \(non-empty\) values for SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET,
and SLACK_APP_TOKEN in conftest.py alongside the other service stubs.
The existing start_socket_mode AsyncMock patch already prevents any real
Slack connection — this change just lets AsyncApp.__init__ succeed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")
Bash(git add app/router/slack.py tests/conftest.py && git status)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<'EOF'
fix: defer AsyncApp creation to start_socket_mode so import never raises BoltError

AsyncApp was instantiated at module level in app/router/slack.py. Any
import of that module \(including the patch\(\) resolver in conftest.py\)
would call AsyncApp\(token=""\) and raise BoltError when SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
is absent — which is always the case in CI.

Move AsyncApp construction + event-handler registration into _build_app\(\),
a private factory called only from start_socket_mode\(\). Importing the
module is now side-effect-free with respect to Slack credentials.

Also revert the unnecessary dummy SLACK_BOT_TOKEN env vars added to
conftest.py in the previous commit — they are no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(docker exec ai-brain python3 -c "
import os, urllib.parse, urllib.request, json

client_id     = os.environ['GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID']
client_secret = os.environ['GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET']
refresh_token = os.environ['GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN']

data = urllib.parse.urlencode\({
    'client_id':     client_id,
    'client_secret': client_secret,
    'refresh_token': refresh_token,
    'grant_type':    'refresh_token',
}\).encode\(\)

req = urllib.request.Request\('https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token', data=data, method='POST'\)
req.add_header\('Content-Type', 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'\)
try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen\(req\) as r:
        resp = json.loads\(r.read\(\)\)
        print\('OK — scopes:', resp.get\('scope'\)\)
        print\('token type:', resp.get\('token_type'\)\)
except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
    print\('ERROR', e.code, json.loads\(e.read\(\)\)\)
")

from app.config import get_settings
s = get_settings\(\)
accs = s.google_accounts
print\('accounts:', len\(accs\)\)
for a in accs:
    print\('  name:', a.get\('name'\)\)
    print\('  client_id:', a.get\('client_id',''\)[:20]+'...'\)
    print\('  refresh_token:', a.get\('refresh_token',''\)[:20]+'...'\)
")
